commands.review_summary:
The branch adds recoverable Tool Search unknown-id suggestions and a focused regression test for guessed tool ids such as `file_write`.

PR surface: Source +54, Tests +30. Total +84 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the source-level failure: current main lets `tool_call` accept a tool id or name and still returns a bare unknown-id error for a guessed missing name. I did not rerun the full model-driven memory flush harness.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] No automated repair is needed; the remaining gate is contributor-supplied real behavior proof plus normal maintainer and CI review.

target.title: fix(agents): suggest recovery for unknown tool ids
target.body:
## Summary

- Fixes #92273 by making Tool Search unknown-id failures actionable instead of returning only a bare `Unknown tool id` message.
- Adds closest-match suggestions for guessed tool ids such as `file_write` when the catalog contains `write`.
- Points the recovery turn to the canonical Tool Search path: `tool_search`, `tool_describe`, then `tool_call` with the exact id or name.

## Behavior or issue addressed

#92273 describes Tool Search `mode: "tools"` breaking pre-compaction memory flush recovery when the model guesses a tool id such as `file_write`. The prior error only said `Unknown tool id: file_write`, leaving the silent recovery turn without enough information to select the managed `write` tool. This patch preserves the original error prefix but adds close matches and explicit recovery steps.

## Real environment tested

Local OpenClaw repository worktree on branch `fix/issue-92273-tool-search-recovery`, using the repository-pinned package manager via `corepack pnpm@11.2.2`.

## Exact steps or command run after this patch

```bash
corepack pnpm@11.2.2 --dir /media/vdb/migrated-from-root-media-vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/issues-83437-92273 exec vitest run src/agents/tool-search.test.ts -t "suggests recoverable Tool Search steps for guessed tool ids|preserves code-mode bridge errors from the child process"
```

```bash
git -C /media/vdb/migrated-from-root-media-vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/issues-83437-92273 diff --check
```

## Evidence after fix

```text
Test Files  2 passed (2)
     Tests  4 passed | 86 skipped (90)
  Duration  9.89s
```

```text
git diff --check
# no output
```

## Observed result after fix

A guessed `file_write` tool id now fails with a recoverable diagnostic that includes `Did you mean: write?` and directs the model to use `tool_search`, `tool_describe`, and `tool_call` with the exact id or name. The target `write` tool is not executed on the failed guessed-id call.

## What was not tested

A full live pre-compaction memory flush run was not executed. The proof is a focused local Tool Search runtime regression covering the unknown-id recovery diagnostic and the existing code-mode bridge error path.

## Risk

Low. The change is scoped to Tool Search unknown-id error formatting. Existing callers that match the `Unknown tool id: ...` prefix continue to see that prefix, while recovery turns receive additional actionable guidance.
target.author: mushuiyu886
